title: Game State, Scenes, Pause, Life Loss, Restart, and Ownership
description: Menus, play, pause, death, and game over are booleans that form impossible combinations; pause leaves timers running; restart retains old projectiles and score. Learners cumulatively produce explicit scene and run-lifecycle state machine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent boot, menu, playing, paused, life-lost, game-over, settings, and exit as explicit valid states.
Define trigger, guard, exit work, entry work, resulting state, and rejected transitions.
Freeze simulation clocks and dangerous input while keeping menus, status, audio control, and window events responsive.
